VIX falling sharply (-10.27% to 21.15) and MOVE falling (-9.92% to 70.03) — equity recovery signal
BoJ hawkish divergence (Ueda reaffirmed hiking cycle, BoJ at 1.0%) — structural driver for 6J
EIA crude inventory BUILD of +3.5M bbl (week ending Feb 27) — bearish CL despite geopolitical risk
Credit spreads narrowing (HY OAS 3.08%) and 2s10s steepening (0.55%) — bullish for RTY and YM
Record gold ETF inflows ($4.5B Feb 2026, $10.5B YTD) — structural support for GC

data_quality.data_freshness: All primary market prices verified as of 2026-03-05T00:32:00Z
data_quality.key_change_vs_prior_report: GDPNow confirmed at 3.0% (ACCELERATING, -1) vs 1730 report's STABLE (0). This changes growth from neutral to negative headwind, reducing ES/NQ/YM/RTY scores by 1 each vs 1730 report. However, real yields now scored FALLING (+2 raw, +4 weighted for ES/NQ) vs 1730 report's RISING (-2 raw, -4 weighted). The net effect is a significant improvement in equity index scores. The 1730 report used rising real yields; this 1932 report correctly uses FALLING real yields per FRED DFII10 data (1.77%, declining from ~2.3% trend).
